title: Introduce path-to-validator obligations with sealed-commit canary lane
acceptance: Phase 1 uses declarative path-to-validator mapping that humans can review.
Full-suite canary runs non-blocking against an exact sealed commit SHA in a clean checkout.
Canary failures produce mapping-gap incidents tied to commit SHA and mapping version.
Symbol-level minimization is deferred until import graph or fs-trace evidence exists.
outOfScope: Unrelated refactors outside the declared scope.
rollbackNotes: Disable the new lane and keep the previous canonical behavior.

importDiagnostics.message: Scope touches 1 module(s) over 600 lines but the card declares no atomizationImpact.extractionCandidates. Extraction-first is the ATM default: propose an atom/atom-map extraction (see .agents/skills/atm-atom-map-refactor), or record disposition "inline" with an inlineReason approved by a human.
